name: The Kraft Heinz Company

profile.description: The Kraft Heinz Company is the 3rd largest food and beverage company in North America, with iconic brands including Kraft, Heinz, Oscar Mayer, Lunchables, Philadelphia, and Velveeta. Revenue of ~$25B annually with 7.1% dividend yield.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Legacy food brands without transformative launches. AI Deployment (0/2): No notable AI deployment. Market Share (1/2): #3 North American food company with iconic brands. Product Pipeline (0/2): No next-gen products. R&D Intensity (1/1): Adequate for packaged foods.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Generic packaged food company purpose. Community Impact (1/2): Some charitable programs. Employee Retention (0/2): Post-3G Capital cuts damaged culture. Insider Buying (1/2): Berkshire Hathaway maintains major stake.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as Dividend. D/E 50.26% (1.0). Forward P/E 10.80 (1.0). ROE -12.58% (0.0). Revenue growth 0.8% (0.69). Profit margin -23.05% (0.0). Revenue 5yr CAGR -1% (0.27). Dividend: 10yr paid (2x), 0 growth years, 0% CAGR (2x), 80% payout.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Physical food manufacturing and distribution is AI-immune. Brand value and shelf space provide moats. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: KHC under 3G Capital influence focused on cost-cutting over innovation — classic finite game behavior.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): No distinctive purpose beyond selling food products.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Average after post-merger cuts stabilized.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Uses Nestle/General Mills as benchmark.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (0/2): Same product portfolio for decades.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): 3G Capital model prioritized cost-cutting over investment.

investment_thesis: KHC offers a 7.1% dividend yield at 10.80x forward P/E with iconic brands (Heinz, Kraft, Oscar Mayer) and Berkshire Hathaway as major shareholder. D/E of 50% is conservative.
thesis_continued: Negative ROE (-12.58%) and profit margin (-23.05%) from goodwill impairments reflect the failed 3G Capital strategy. Revenue essentially flat (0.8% YoY). No dividend growth in 5 years. 80% payout ratio limits flexibility. Consumer shift away from processed foods is structural.
key_catalysts: Stabilization of organic revenue growth above inflation
Cost restructuring improving margins from current negative levels
7.1% dividend yield providing income at deep value entry point
key_risks: Negative ROE and profit margins reflect structural impairment issues
Zero dividend growth for 5 years with 80% payout ratio — any earnings decline threatens the dividend
Consumer shift from processed to fresh/healthier foods creating secular headwinds for legacy brands
sources: Kraft Heinz Q1 2026 Earnings — SEC Filings, Yahoo Finance, 2026
